talk_title: Spatial Transcriptomics Profiling of Colorectal Cancer Tumors in Alaska Native People: Discovery of Prognostic Biomarkers

biomarkers.name: SPINK4
biomarkers.finding: Upregulated in epithelial compartment of tumor center in patients who did not die from colorectal cancer.

biomarkers.name: MUQ2
biomarkers.finding: Upregulated in epithelial compartment of tumor center in patients who did not die from colorectal cancer.

biomarkers.name: HBA2
biomarkers.finding: Upregulated in epithelial compartment of invasive margin in patients who die from colorectal cancer.

biomarkers.name: Interferon alpha and gamma response pathway
biomarkers.finding: Enriched in epithelial compartment (tumor center and invasive margin) in patients who did not die from colorectal cancer.

biomarkers.name: Epithelial-mesenchymal transition pathways
biomarkers.finding: Enriched in epithelial and stromal compartments of invasive margin in patients who die from colorectal cancer.

biomarkers.name: Plasma cells
biomarkers.finding: Increased in epithelial compartment at tumor center in patients who did not die from colorectal cancer.

biomarkers.name: Mast cells
biomarkers.finding: Increased in stromal compartment at tumor center in patients who die from colorectal cancer.

models.name: Alaska Native colorectal cancer patients
models.genotype_or_key_features: 217 patients with FFPE tissue blocks, matched for sex, tumor stage, and tumor sites. Deaths matched 1:2 to non-deaths.

MOA_summary: This study utilized spatial transcriptomics (GeoMix) to profile colorectal cancer tumors in Alaska Native people, identifying prognostic biomarkers associated with mortality. Key findings include differential gene expression (SPINK4, MUQ2, HBA2) and pathway enrichment (interferon response, EMT) in tumor and stromal compartments, as well as altered cell type abundance (plasma cells, mast cells), correlating with patient survival outcomes.
key_findings: Identified spatially specific markers tied to colorectal cancer-specific mortality in Alaska Native people.
In the tumor center epithelial compartment, downregulation of MILK2 and SPINK4, and interferon alpha and gamma pathways were associated with not dying from colorectal cancer.
In the tumor center, a decrease in plasma cells and an increase in mast cells were observed in patients who did not die from colorectal cancer.
In the invasive margin epithelial compartment, an increase in HBA2 expression was observed in patients who died from colorectal cancer.
Epithelial-mesenchymal transition pathways were enriched in the invasive margin (epithelial and stromal compartments) in patients who died from colorectal cancer.

talk_title: Highly Multiplexed Immunoassays Enable Discovery of Multi-Marker EV Surface Signatures as Novel Biomarkers

biomarkers.name: CEA (soluble)
biomarkers.finding: 16% of stage IV CRC patients were undetectable.

biomarkers.name: CEA on EV surface
biomarkers.finding: Detectable in stage IV CRC patients where soluble CEA was undetectable.

biomarkers.name: CD73, CD13, CEA triplet signature
biomarkers.finding: Recurrent components in CRC-secreted EVs, with prognostic value.

biomarkers.name: Epithelial cell markers (CEA, E-cadherin, EpCAM)
biomarkers.finding: Reflect epithelial cell origin in CRC EVs.

biomarkers.name: Stromal cell markers
biomarkers.finding: Reflect stromal cell origin in CRC EVs.

biomarkers.name: CD73
biomarkers.finding: Found on both stromal and epithelial populations.

models.name: Colorectal cancer patients
models.genotype_or_key_features: Three independent cohorts: 1 cross-sectional (Bob Coffey lab), 2 longitudinal (Andy Nixon lab).

models.name: Diffi, HCT15
models.genotype_or_key_features: Colorectal carcinoma cell lines used for assay validation.

MOA_summary: This presentation introduces a novel combinatorial immunosequencing platform for highly multiplexed profiling of extracellular vesicle (EV) surface signatures. The technology enables the discovery of multi-marker combinations that can distinguish cell origins and provide prognostic information, demonstrating improved sensitivity over single markers and showing consistent findings across multiple colorectal cancer cohorts.
key_findings: Soluble CEA was undetectable in 16% of stage IV CRC patients, while the same molecule on EV surfaces was detectable.
The combinatorial immunosequencing platform can detect rare EV subpopulations (1 in 10 million) with high sensitivity and a four-log dynamic range.
Three-marker combinations consistently discriminated between epithelial and stromal cell origins in CRC EVs across three independent cohorts.
Several multi-marker EV surface signatures showed FDR significant prognostic value in longitudinal CRC cohorts.
CD73, CD13, and CEA were identified as recurrent components in CRC-secreted EVs with prognostic potential.
open_questions: Potential for early detection using multi-marker EV signatures.

talk_title: Differences in Survival Outcomes Among Metastatic Colorectal Cancer Patients Across Fibroblast Growth Factor Receptor Two and Three Expression Levels

biomarkers.name: FGFR2 gene expression
biomarkers.finding: Associated with worse overall survival across treatment arms (HR 1.28, median survival difference 7.4 months).

biomarkers.name: FGFR2 gene expression
biomarkers.finding: Predictive of overall survival in patients treated with cetuximab (HR 1.56, median survival difference 12 months).

biomarkers.name: FGFR3 gene expression
biomarkers.finding: Predictive of overall survival in patients treated with cetuximab (HR 0.59, median survival difference 12.4 months).

biomarkers.name: FGFR2 SNP RS-3135718
biomarkers.finding: Predictive of progression-free and overall survival in patients treated with cetuximab. Homozygous variant associated with improved survival (median survival difference 3 months vs WT).

models.name: CALGB SWOG 80405 Phase III clinical trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Newly diagnosed metastatic colorectal cancer patients randomized to chemotherapy + cetuximab or chemotherapy + bevacizumab. Covariates adjusted for: KRAS mutation, BRAF mutation, MSI status, sidedness, age, sex.

models.name: CRC cell lines
models.genotype_or_key_features: Used for preclinical validation of FGFR2 inhibition.

clinical_data.metric: Median Survival Difference (FGFR2 high vs low)
Overall Survival (FGFR2 high vs low, cetuximab arm)
Median Survival Difference (FGFR2 high vs low, cetuximab arm)
Overall Survival (FGFR3 high vs low, cetuximab arm)
Median Survival Difference (FGFR3 high vs low, cetuximab arm)
Overall Survival (FGFR2 SNP RS-3135718 homozygous variant vs WT, cetuximab arm)

MOA_summary: This study investigated the prognostic and predictive value of FGFR2 and FGFR3 gene expression and FGFR2 SNPs in metastatic colorectal cancer patients from the CALGB SWOG 80405 trial. FGFR2 high expression was associated with worse survival overall and specifically in the cetuximab arm, while FGFR3 high expression and a specific FGFR2 SNP (RS-3135718) were associated with improved survival in the cetuximab arm, suggesting differential roles in response to EGFR inhibition.
key_findings: FGFR2 high expression was prognostic for worse overall survival in mCRC patients (HR 1.28).
FGFR2 high expression was predictive of worse overall survival in the cetuximab arm (HR 1.56).
FGFR3 high expression was predictive of improved overall survival in the cetuximab arm (HR 0.59).
FGFR2 SNP RS-3135718 variant allele was predictive of improved PFS and OS in the cetuximab arm.
FGFR2 inhibition decreased cell viability in CRC cell lines, reiterating its criticality for cell survival.
open_questions: Why do FGFR2 and FGFR3 behave differently despite being in the same family?
Underlying mechanisms for FGFR3's association with improved survival in the cetuximab arm.

talk_title: Mapping of Spatial Organization of B Cells and Immune Signaling in Triple Negative Breast Cancer to Characterize Response to Immunotherapy

biomarkers.name: Germinal center B cells
biomarkers.finding: Associated with immunotherapy response on-treatment.

biomarkers.name: Plasma memory B cells
biomarkers.finding: Associated with immunotherapy response on-treatment.

biomarkers.name: Naive B cells
biomarkers.finding: Associated with immunotherapy response on-treatment.

biomarkers.name: B activated niche
biomarkers.finding: Significantly associated with immunotherapy response across neighborhood scales.

biomarkers.name: Germinal center niche
biomarkers.finding: Trends towards association with immunotherapy response.

biomarkers.name: Plasma-maturing niche
biomarkers.finding: Trends towards association with immunotherapy response.

biomarkers.name: Plasma-rich niche interacting with CD15, MHC-1 and 2 epithelial cells
biomarkers.finding: Associated with immunotherapy response at baseline (immunotherapy arm).

biomarkers.name: B cells from B activated niche interacting with CD4, CD8, plasma, and B cells
biomarkers.finding: Associated with immunotherapy response on-treatment.

biomarkers.name: Naive B cells enrichment
biomarkers.finding: Most abundant phenotype enriched on-treatment in chemoimmunotherapy arm, also enriched in chemotherapy arm.

biomarkers.name: Cytokine enrichment within niches
biomarkers.finding: Persistent feature in chemoimmunotherapy responders post-treatment.

models.name: NEOCHIP study
models.genotype_or_key_features: 280 early-stage triple-negative breast cancer patients randomized to chemoimmunotherapy or chemotherapy. Samples collected at baseline, on-treatment, and post-treatment.

MOA_summary: This study investigated the role of B cells and cytokine signaling in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) response to immunotherapy using imaging mass cytometry (IMC). Findings indicate that B cell densities, specific B cell niches (e.g., B activated niche), and their interactions with other immune cells are significantly associated with immunotherapy response. The spatial organization of B cells and cytokine enrichment within niches appear to be key drivers of treatment efficacy.
key_findings: Several B cell populations (germinal center B, plasma memory, naive B) are associated with immunotherapy response on-treatment.
Specific B cell niches (germinal center, B activated, plasma-rich, plasma-maturing) were identified.
The B activated niche and its interactions with other immune cells are predictive of immunotherapy response.
B cells are more significantly impacted post-immunotherapy compared to other TME cells.
Cytokine enrichment within niches is a persistent feature in immunotherapy responders across treatment time points.
open_questions: How do B cells and plasma cells contribute to immunotherapy response, given the traditional focus on T cells?
The role of antibodies produced by plasma cells in immunotherapy response.
Potential for targeting B cells or their niches in immunotherapy.

talk_title: AI-Powered Deep Visual Proteomics (DVP) Links Pancreatic Precursor Biology to PDAC Survival Phenotypes

biomarkers.name: Cancer-associated normal ducts vs. incidental normal ducts
biomarkers.finding: Cancer-associated ducts show enrichment of proteins reflecting oxidative stress, metabolic reprogramming, and immune editing.

biomarkers.name: Inflammation and antigen presentation pathways
biomarkers.finding: Upregulated early in normal ducts, all the way to low-grade PANA, then sharply drop in high-grade PANA, suggesting immune evasion.

biomarkers.name: KRAS mutations
biomarkers.finding: Identified hotspot mutations in normal ducts, ADMs, and PANA lesions, allowing genotype-phenotype correlation.

models.name: Pancreatic precursor lesions and PDAC cohort
models.genotype_or_key_features: Cohort 1: Incidental normal ducts and ADMs, low-grade PANAs. Cohort 2: Cancer-associated normal ducts, low-grade and high-grade PANAs, tumor cells. Isolated <100 phenotype-matched epithelial cells.

MOA_summary: This study applied AI-powered Deep Visual Proteomics (DVP) to analyze pancreatic precursor lesions and PDAC. By integrating high-resolution microscopy with mass spectrometry-based spatial proteomics, the researchers identified early molecular changes in normal ducts associated with cancer development, including immune editing and metabolic reprogramming. The technology also enabled the detection of KRAS mutations directly from proteomic data, linking genotype to phenotype and revealing insights into immune evasion in high-grade PANA.
key_findings: Cancer-associated normal ducts show distinct proteomic profiles compared to incidental normal ducts, including oxidative stress, metabolic reprogramming, and immune editing.
Inflammation and antigen presentation pathways are upregulated early in pancreatic precursor lesions but drop sharply in high-grade PANA, indicating immune evasion.
KRAS hotspot mutations can be identified from proteomic data, enabling genotype-phenotype correlation in precursor lesions.
The study identified a 'cancer field effect' where normal ducts near tumors acquire immune signatures.
DVP identified distinct proteomic differences between spatially adjacent regions within tumors, highlighting tumor heterogeneity.
open_questions: Translating these findings into clinical diagnostics or therapeutics.
Further exploration of the identified immune evasion mechanisms.

talk_title: Spatial Immune Multiomics Refines Prognostication in Early-Stage Estrogen Receptor-Positive Breast Cancer

speaker.name: Zach Kinsella
speaker.affiliation: Royal College of Surgeons in Ireland

targets.dependency_context: inflamed epithelia in ER+ breast cancer
inflamed epithelia and stroma in ER+ breast cancer
inflamed epithelia in ER+ breast cancer
inflamed epithelia in ER+ breast cancer
intermediate recurrence score ER+ breast cancer, chemo arm

biomarkers.name: Inflamed TME and epithelia
biomarkers.finding: Associated with poorer outcome in patients receiving chemotherapy in the intermediate recurrence score group.

biomarkers.name: Stromal CD8 density
biomarkers.finding: Predictive marker of chemo inferiority in intermediate recurrence score ER+ breast cancer.

biomarkers.name: Epithelial CD8 density
biomarkers.finding: Conferred poorer outcome in patients randomized to chemotherapy.

biomarkers.name: Tregs fluidity
biomarkers.finding: More fluid in inflamed TME, likely to infiltrate epithelia.

models.name: Irish patients from TAILORx trial
models.genotype_or_key_features: Early-stage ER-positive, HER2-negative breast cancer patients with intermediate recurrence score (11-25). Randomized to hormone therapy alone or chemoendocrine therapy.

clinical_data.population: Intermediate recurrence score ER+ breast cancer

clinical_data.metric: Median survival time difference (90th percentile CD8 density)
clinical_data.population: Intermediate recurrence score ER+ breast cancer

MOA_summary: This study utilized spatial multiomics (IHC, spatial transcriptomics, spatial proteomics) on early-stage ER+ breast cancer patients from the TAILORx trial to refine prognostication in the intermediate recurrence score group. Findings reveal that inflamed immune microenvironments are associated with dysfunctional responses, including upregulation of checkpoints and downregulation of ER signaling. High stromal CD8 density was identified as a predictive marker of chemo inferiority, suggesting that immune infiltration patterns can refine treatment decisions.
key_findings: Four TME types and three epithelial types were identified, with immune types not significantly captured by Oncotype DX Recurrence Score.
Inflamed epithelia were characterized by downregulation of ESR1 and suppression of DNA repair pathways.
Inflamed TME and epithelia were prognostic in the intermediate recurrence score group, with chemotherapy showing poorer outcomes.
High stromal CD8 density was a predictive marker of chemo inferiority in the intermediate recurrence score group.
Increasing CD8 density conferred poorer outcome on chemotherapy, adding prognostic information to clinical variables.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Inflamed states are transcriptomically wired to more dysfunctions, such as expression of checkpoints, exhaustion transcripts, M2-like macrophage, and remodeling genes, as well as dedifferentiation.
open_questions: How to translate these findings into a clinical assay, potentially using foundational models for TIL scoring.
The role of chemotherapy in ER+ breast cancer with high CD8 density.
The relationship between CD8 density and response to immunotherapy in triple-negative breast cancer.

talk_title: Unbiased Phenotyping of CRISPR Edited Tumors with Preserved Map and Subcellular Imaging of the Mouse Whole Transcriptome, 22,000 Plex

targets.dependency_context: mouse lung tumors treated with PD1

biomarkers.name: Predicted human CCAM6 expression
biomarkers.finding: Identified specific tumors with high predicted human CCAM6 expression in mouse models, correlating with a switch in histology resembling human CCAM6-high tumors.

biomarkers.name: STK11 knockout phenotype
biomarkers.finding: Associated with cold TME and fibrotic signature.

biomarkers.name: beta2m knockout phenotype
biomarkers.finding: Associated with low T cells and cold TME.

biomarkers.name: beta-catenin knockout phenotype
biomarkers.finding: Associated with hot TME and T cell activity.

biomarkers.name: SOX1 knockout phenotype
biomarkers.finding: Associated with hot TME and T cell activity.

biomarkers.name: PTPN2 knockout phenotype
biomarkers.finding: Associated with hot TME and T cell activity.

models.name: PertiMap platform
models.genotype_or_key_features: Scaled in vivo perturbation platform using protein barcodes (ProCode CRISPRs) in mouse lungs. Generated >600 genetic perturbations across 4 therapeutic interventions.

MOA_summary: This presentation describes the PertiMap platform, which uses CRISPR-edited, barcoded tumors in mice to generate large-scale in vivo perturbation data. This data is then 'humanized' using the MAIRS model, which projects human biology onto mouse H&E images, enabling the discovery of novel therapeutic targets and combinations. The approach allows for unbiased phenotyping of genetic perturbations and identification of drivers of tumor biology and response to therapy.
key_findings: The PertiMap platform enables scaled in vivo genetic perturbation and multimodal tissue profiling.
The MAIRS model can project human biology onto mouse H&E images, effectively 'humanizing' preclinical models.
MAIRS identified tumors with high predicted human CCAM6 expression in mouse models, correlating with specific histological changes.
The embedding space of perturbations revealed distinct phenotypic clusters associated with immune infiltration, fibrosis, and metabolic signatures.
VEGFR1 knockout tumors treated with PD1 showed a shift towards a hot TME, suggesting a rationale for combination therapy.

combination_strategies.rationale: VEGFR1 knockout tumors treated with PD1 showed a drastic shift towards a hot TME.
Identified a gene knockout that suggests a rationale for combination with PD1.

open_questions: How to validate the predicted human gene expression in mouse models.
The application of MAIRS to humanized mouse models with both mouse and human cells.
The number of genes that can be identified in the inferred spatial transcriptomes.
